question: Can one of three predeclared 2h, 4h, or 8h maximum holding horizons repair the current one-shot BTCUSDT Donchian/ATR strategy proxy's post-cost mean return while entry, stop, take-profit, direction choice, and all evidence gates remain fixed?
development_gate: candidate only; trades >= 100; base and stress mean > 0; at least two of 2021/2022/2023 base means > 0; worst annual base mean >= -0.001; base p01 >= -0.02; base worst >= -0.05; rank by worst annual base mean, stress mean, base mean, then shorter hold; keep at most one
evaluation_gate: fixed candidate trades >= 60; base and stress means > 0; 2024 and 2025 base means > 0; base improvement over same-direction 4x15m benchmark >= 0.0005; base p01 >= -0.02; base worst >= -0.05
confirmation_gate: fixed candidate trades >= 15; base and stress means > 0; base p01 >= -0.02; base worst >= -0.05; trade-count-weighted evaluation plus confirmation base mean > 0
known_exposure: All underlying 2021-2026H1 BTCUSDT data are exposed. The current 4x15m family and an older 96x15m proxy are exposed. The exact fixed-rule 8/16/32x15m candidates have not been run before this checkpoint, so evaluation is only a rule-level sequential holdout.
allowed_fixes_after_checkpoint: parsing, timestamp, checksum and data-quality defects
implementation defects that do not alter candidates, periods, costs, event order, fixed entry/exit rules, ranks or gates
forbidden_after_checkpoint: adding or removing hold horizons after viewing results
changing entry, stop, take-profit, costs, periods, ranks or gates
reading evaluation before exactly one development candidate is fixed
reading confirmation before the fixed candidate passes evaluation
